answer:
**Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 2 (Vyavahārādhyāya)** lays down this exact graduated monthly interest schedule.[^1][^2]

> An eightieth part is the interest per month when there is a pledge; otherwise, it is two, three, four, or five percent, according to the order of the *varṇas*. // Yj_2.37 //[^1]  
>   
> Those who travel through forests may pay ten percent, and those who travel by sea, twenty percent; or all persons of all castes may pay the interest they have agreed upon. // Yj_2.38 //[^2]

The immediately following verse in the same passage caps the maximum recoverable interest at eight times the principal for liquids; four times for cloth and grain (noted as "cloth, grain"); and twice the principal for gold.[^3]

[^1]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 2, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-286928).
[^2]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 2, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-36c790).
[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 2, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-73b94c).

args.query: "eight times" liquids OR "four times" cloth OR "three times" grain OR "twice" gold principal interest
"per month" OR "monthly" pledge OR security interest
"ten percent" OR "twenty percent" OR 10% OR 20% forest OR sea OR ocean travel interest
